name: Nonlinearity
description: Hubs, loops, and gateways exist where intended and matter to play. Nonlinearity is deliberate, not decorative. Choices are meaningfully different.

rubric.instructions: Evaluate whether nonlinear elements are meaningful. Hubs should fan out, loops should return with difference, gateways should matter.

criteria.name: hub_significance
criteria.description: Hubs fan out to genuinely different content, not cosmetic variations.

good: Hub offers 3 paths with distinct consequences
bad: Hub has 3 paths that all lead to same outcome

criteria.name: loop_difference
criteria.description: Loops return with meaningful difference via codeword/state changes.

good: Returning players see different dialogue based on prior choices

criteria.name: first_choice_integrity
criteria.description: Early choices avoid funnels where siblings are functionally equivalent. Convergence is acknowledged in subsequent prose.

good: First paragraph after convergence reflects the entering path
Choices feel distinct even if converging later
bad: Three choices all lead to same unchanged scene

failure_guidance: Add outcome differences to cosmetic loops. Add branches with distinct outcomes to thin hubs or merge if unnecessary. Ensure convergence scenes acknowledge the entering path with lexical, behavioral, or situational differences.
